successor: Intel (1999); later Dialogic Group (2013, spun out from Intel via private equity)
notes: Acquired by Intel for ~$780M in 1999. Spun out by Intel in 2013. Acquired Cantata/Brooktrout via EAS Group in 2007. Still active as Dialogic Inc.
Acquired by Harte-Hanks in 2001; brand subsequently owned by Informa. Study author.
Acquired Lucent's CT division in 1998. Acquired by EAS Group in 2005; merged into Cantata Technology. Cantata acquired by Dialogic Group in 2007.
CT hardware and software vendor. CT Access product competed with CT Media. Eventually absorbed into Comverse Technology.
MediaPath was Mitel's server-based PBX product. Company remained independent through multiple acquisitions; Enghouse Systems acquired Mitel in 2022.
Maker of RealCT, a proprietary C++ CT development environment. Acquired by Dialogic or absorbed into the market consolidation of late 1990s.
Industry consortium that developed S.100, H.100, S.300, S.400 CT standards. Largely superseded by VoIP/SIP standards bodies (IETF, ITU-T) in early 2000s.
